title: Operator Hold Boundary Cadence Surface Execution
objective: Land a repo-owned operator cadence surface for the current Olares hold boundary so minimal MCP verification and deferred Operations Visibility reopen checks can be rerun from one governed command.
dependency_note: Packet 054 revalidated the admitted AI boundary and Packet 055 formalized the data-driven hold. The next truthful move was to reduce operator drag on that boundary without reopening product work or widening AI admission.
execution_steps: Add a deferred-view count helper that can prefer an authoritative live DSN while degrading honestly on the local development database.
Add PowerShell and Bash wrappers that combine minimal MCP trio verification with the deferred-view check.
Wire the new cadence surface into the repo task and runbook surfaces.
Validate the wrapper locally and record the truthful outcome.
constraints: Do not reopen `v_resource_allocation` or `v_equipment_needs` consumers while they remain empty.
Do not widen AI-services admission beyond the admitted minimal MCP trio plus `apex-jobs`.
Do not pretend the local `.env.dev` development database is authoritative for the live `09` Operations Visibility hold decision.

local_wrapper_result.decision: Authoritative deferred view counts require a live DSN such as SEAM_DATABASE_URL; the local development database is not sufficient for this hold check.

execution_rationale: Packet 055 created an explicit hold boundary, but the repo still lacked a single command for rerunning the two current trigger classes.
A helper that returns `UNAVAILABLE` on the local development database is more truthful than silently converting that environment into a false hold verdict.
This packet reduces operator relay burden while preserving the exact current trust and scope boundaries.
next_packet_candidate: Use the new cadence surface with an authoritative live DSN when the deferred Operations Visibility reopen trigger needs a fresh check, or reopen only a different bounded Olares lane with stronger evidence.
closure_note: Executed 2026-05-06 as the bounded operator follow-on to the Packet 055 hold decision. Packet 056 landed a single repo-owned cadence surface for minimal MCP verify plus deferred-view checks, and local validation proved that the helper reports `UNAVAILABLE` rather than a false hold verdict when only the `.env.dev` development database is available.
next_action: Rerun the new cadence surface with `SEAM_DATABASE_URL` or another authoritative live DSN when a real deferred-view reopen decision is needed.
